# Run a Langfuse Experiment with Item-Level Evaluators (TypeScript)

## Background

https://langfuse.com/ is an open-source AI engineering platform that provides observability, prompt management, and evaluation for LLM applications. The TypeScript SDK's `experiment.run` API loops a task function over a dataset, automatically traces every execution, and lets you attach **item-level evaluators** whose results are emitted as scores on each trace in Langfuse.

In this task you will build a small Node.js (TypeScript) project that runs a Langfuse experiment over a **local** dataset using the modular JS/TS SDK packages, with a custom task function and two custom evaluators, and then record the resulting trace IDs so they can be inspected via the Langfuse API.

## Requirements

* Create a TypeScript Node.js project at `/home/user/myproject` that uses the modular Langfuse JS/TS SDK packages (`@langfuse/client`, `@langfuse/otel`, `@opentelemetry/sdk-node`).
* Initialize OpenTelemetry with `LangfuseSpanProcessor` before running the experiment so traces are exported to Langfuse.
* Define an in-process **local** dataset of exactly 3 items, each with an `input` string and an `expectedOutput` string.
* Define a task function that returns the uppercase form of the item's `input` string. The task function must **not** call any external LLM provider.
* Define **two** item-level evaluators that are passed to `experiment.run` via the `evaluators` option:
  * One named `accuracy` that returns numeric value `1` when the task output exactly equals `expectedOutput` (case-sensitive) and `0` otherwise.
  * One named `response_length` that returns the number of characters of the task `output`.
* Run the experiment via `langfuse.experiment.run({...})`. The experiment `name` MUST be `harbor-experiment-${ZEALT_RUN_ID}`, where `ZEALT_RUN_ID` is read from the environment variable of the same name.
* After the experiment completes, ensure all spans are delivered to Langfuse (e.g. by shutting down the OpenTelemetry SDK) and then write a log file describing the run.

## Implementation Hints

* Read `LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY`, `LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY`, and `LANGFUSE_BASE_URL` from the environment — the Langfuse SDK and the OTel processor pick them up automatically.
* `LangfuseSpanProcessor` must be wired into a Node OpenTelemetry SDK and started **before** the experiment runs; calling `await otelSdk.shutdown()` at the end is the supported way to flush all spans for short-lived scripts.
* The `ExperimentResult` returned by `experiment.run` exposes per-item results (`itemResults[]`), and each item result includes the Langfuse `traceId` of the trace that was created for that item.
* The task may be implemented as plain TypeScript executed with `ts-node`/`tsx` (or compiled with `tsc` and then run with `node`) — either approach is acceptable.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: `/home/user/myproject`
* The executor is responsible for actually running the script so that the experiment runs end-to-end and traces & scores are delivered to Langfuse.
* Log file: `/home/user/myproject/output.log`
* The experiment name must be `harbor-experiment-${ZEALT_RUN_ID}` where `ZEALT_RUN_ID` is read from the environment variable of the same name.
* The log file MUST contain the following lines (each on its own line, in any order):
  * One line in the exact format: `Experiment name: <experiment_name>`
  * Exactly 3 lines in the exact format: `Trace ID: <trace_id>` — one per dataset item, in the order the items were processed. Each `<trace_id>` must be a non-empty trace ID returned by Langfuse for that item.
* The script must use the Langfuse JS/TS SDK's `experiment.run` API (i.e. `langfuse.experiment.run({...})`) on a **local** dataset; do not create a Langfuse-hosted dataset for this task.
* After the script exits, each recorded trace must exist in Langfuse and have **both** of the following scores attached: a score named `accuracy` and a score named `response_length`.

# Run a Langfuse Experiment with Item-Level Evaluators (TypeScript)

## Background

https://langfuse.com/ is an open-source AI engineering platform that provides observability, prompt management, and evaluation for LLM applications. The TypeScript SDK's `experiment.run` API loops a task function over a dataset, automatically traces every execution, and lets you attach **item-level evaluators** whose results are emitted as scores on each trace in Langfuse.

In this task you will build a small Node.js (TypeScript) project that runs a Langfuse experiment over a **local** dataset using the modular JS/TS SDK packages, with a custom task function and two custom evaluators, and then record the resulting trace IDs so they can be inspected via the Langfuse API.

## Requirements

* Create a TypeScript Node.js project at `/home/user/myproject` that uses the modular Langfuse JS/TS SDK packages (`@langfuse/client`, `@langfuse/otel`, `@opentelemetry/sdk-node`).
* Initialize OpenTelemetry with `LangfuseSpanProcessor` before running the experiment so traces are exported to Langfuse.
* Define an in-process **local** dataset of exactly 3 items, each with an `input` string and an `expectedOutput` string.
* Define a task function that returns the uppercase form of the item's `input` string. The task function must **not** call any external LLM provider.
* Define **two** item-level evaluators that are passed to `experiment.run` via the `evaluators` option:
  * One named `accuracy` that returns numeric value `1` when the task output exactly equals `expectedOutput` (case-sensitive) and `0` otherwise.
  * One named `response_length` that returns the number of characters of the task `output`.
* Run the experiment via `langfuse.experiment.run({...})`. The experiment `name` MUST be `harbor-experiment-${ZEALT_RUN_ID}`, where `ZEALT_RUN_ID` is read from the environment variable of the same name.
* After the experiment completes, ensure all spans are delivered to Langfuse (e.g. by shutting down the OpenTelemetry SDK) and then write a log file describing the run.

## Implementation Hints

* Read `LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY`, `LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY`, and `LANGFUSE_BASE_URL` from the environment — the Langfuse SDK and the OTel processor pick them up automatically.
* `LangfuseSpanProcessor` must be wired into a Node OpenTelemetry SDK and started **before** the experiment runs; calling `await otelSdk.shutdown()` at the end is the supported way to flush all spans for short-lived scripts.
* The `ExperimentResult` returned by `experiment.run` exposes per-item results (`itemResults[]`), and each item result includes the Langfuse `traceId` of the trace that was created for that item.
* The task may be implemented as plain TypeScript executed with `ts-node`/`tsx` (or compiled with `tsc` and then run with `node`) — either approach is acceptable.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: `/home/user/myproject`
* The executor is responsible for actually running the script so that the experiment runs end-to-end and traces & scores are delivered to Langfuse.
* Log file: `/home/user/myproject/output.log`
* The experiment name must be `harbor-experiment-${ZEALT_RUN_ID}` where `ZEALT_RUN_ID` is read from the environment variable of the same name.
* The log file MUST contain the following lines (each on its own line, in any order):
  * One line in the exact format: `Experiment name: <experiment_name>`
  * Exactly 3 lines in the exact format: `Trace ID: <trace_id>` — one per dataset item, in the order the items were processed. Each `<trace_id>` must be a non-empty trace ID returned by Langfuse for that item.
* The script must use the Langfuse JS/TS SDK's `experiment.run` API (i.e. `langfuse.experiment.run({...})`) on a **local** dataset; do not create a Langfuse-hosted dataset for this task.
* After the script exits, each recorded trace must exist in Langfuse and have **both** of the following scores attached: a score named `accuracy` and a score named `response_length`.

private createDatasetItemLinkFunction;

/**
 * Manages the execution and evaluation of experiments on datasets.
 *
 * The ExperimentManager provides a comprehensive framework for running experiments
 * that test models or tasks against datasets, with support for automatic evaluation,
 * scoring.
 *
 * @example Basic experiment usage
 * ```typescript
 * const langfuse = new LangfuseClient();
 *
 * const result = await langfuse.experiment.run({
 *   name: "Capital Cities Test",
 *   description: "Testing model knowledge of world capitals",
 *   data: [
 *     { input: "France", expectedOutput: "Paris" },
 *     { input: "Germany", expectedOutput: "Berlin" }
 *   ],
 *   task: async ({ input }) => {
 *     const response = await openai.chat.completions.create({
 *       model: "gpt-4",
 *       messages: [{ role: "user", content: `What is the capital of ${input}?` }]
 *     });
 *     return response.choices[0].message.content;
 *   },
 *   evaluators: [
 *     async ({ input, output, expectedOutput }) => ({
 *       name: "exact_match",
 *       value: output === expectedOutput ? 1 : 0
 *     })
 *   ]
 * });
 *
 * console.log(await result.format());
 * ```
 *
 * @example Using with Langfuse datasets
 * ```typescript
 * const dataset = await langfuse.dataset.get("my-dataset");
 *
 * const result = await dataset.runExperiment({
 *   name: "Model Comparison",
 *   task: myTask,
 *   evaluators: [myEvaluator],
 *   runEvaluators: [averageScoreEvaluator]
 * });
 * ```
 *
 * @public
 */
declare class ExperimentManager {
    private langfuseClient;
    /**
     * Creates a new ExperimentManager instance.
     *
     * @param params - Configuration object
     * @param params.langfuseClient - The Langfuse client instance for API communication
     * @internal
     */
    constructor(params: {
        langfuseClient: LangfuseClient;
    });
    /**
     * Gets the global logger instance for experiment-related logging.
     *
     * @returns The global logger instance
     * @internal
     */
    get logger(): _langfuse_core.Logger;
    /**
     * Executes an experiment by running a task on each data item and evaluating the results.
     *
     * This method orchestrates the complete experiment lifecycle:
     * 1. Executes the task function on each data item with proper tracing
     * 2. Runs item-level evaluators on each task output
     * 3. Executes run-level evaluators on the complete result set
     * 4. Links results to dataset runs (for Langfuse datasets)
     * 5. Stores all scores and traces in Langfuse
     *
     * @param config - The experiment configuration
     * @param config.name - Human-readable name for the experiment
     * @param config.runName - Optional exact name for the experiment run (defaults to name + timestamp)
     * @param config.description - Optional description of the experiment's purpose
     * @param config.metadata - Optional metadata to attach to the experiment run
     * @param config.data - Array of data items to process (ExperimentItem[] or DatasetItem[])
     * @param config.task - Function that processes each data item and returns output
     * @param config.evaluators - Optional array of functions to evaluate each item's output
     * @param config.runEvaluators - Optional array of functions to evaluate the entire run
     * @param config.maxConcurrency - Maximum number of concurrent task executions (default: 50)
     *
     * @returns Promise that resolves to experiment results including:
     *   - experimentId: Stable identifier for the experiment execution
     *   - runName: The experiment run name (either provided or generated)
     *   - itemResults: Results for each processed data item
     *   - runEvaluations: Results from run-level evaluators
     *   - datasetRunId: ID of the dataset run (if using Langfuse datasets)
     *   - format: Function to format results for display
     *
     * @throws {Error} When task execution fails and cannot be handled gracefully
     * @throws {Error} When required evaluators fail critically
     *
     * @example Simple experiment
     * ```typescript
     * const result = await langfuse.experiment.run({
     *   name: "Translation Quality Test",
     *   data: [
     *     { input: "Hello world", expectedOutput: "Hola mundo" },
     *     { input: "Good morning", expectedOutput: "Buenos días" }
     *   ],
     *   task: async ({ input }) => translateText(input, 'es'),
     *   evaluators: [
     *     async ({ output, expectedOutput }) => ({
     *       name: "bleu_score",
     *       value: calculateBleuScore(output, expectedOutput)
     *     })
     *   ]
     * });
     * ```
     *
     * @example Experiment with concurrency control
     * ```typescript
     * const result = await langfuse.experiment.run({
     *   name: "Large Scale Evaluation",
     *   data: largeBatchOfItems,
     *   task: expensiveModelCall,
     *   maxConcurrency: 5, // Process max 5 items simultaneously
     *   evaluators: [myEvaluator],
     *   runEvaluators: [
     *     async ({ itemResults }) => ({
     *       name: "average_score",
     *       value: itemResults.reduce((acc, r) => acc + r.evaluations[0].value, 0) / itemResults.length
     *     })
     *   ]
     * });
     * ```
     *
     * @see {@link ExperimentParams} for detailed parameter documentation
     * @see {@link ExperimentResult} for detailed return value documentation
     * @see {@link Evaluator} for evaluator function specifications
     * @see {@link RunEvaluator} for run evaluator function specifications
     *
     * @public
     */
    run<Input = any, ExpectedOutput = any, Metadata extends Record<string, any> = Record<string, any>>(config: ExperimentParams<Input, ExpectedOutput, Metadata>): Promise<ExperimentResult<Input, ExpectedOutput, Metadata>>;
    /**
     * Executes the task and evaluators for a single data item.
     *
     * This method handles the complete processing pipeline for one data item:
     * 1. Executes the task within a traced observation span
     * 2. Links the result to a dataset run (if applicable)
     * 3. Runs all item-level evaluators on the output
     * 4. Stores evaluation scores in Langfuse
     * 5. Handles errors gracefully by continuing with remaining evaluators
     *
     * @param params - Parameters for item execution
     * @param params.experimentName - Name of the parent experiment
     * @param params.experimentRunName - Run name for the parent experiment
     * @param params.experimentDescription - Description of the parent experiment

* and omits the standard parameters (input, output, expected) that are
 * handled by the adapter, leaving only the additional configuration parameters.
 *
 * @template E - The AutoEvals evaluator function type
 * @internal
 */
type Params<E> = Parameters<E extends (...args: any[]) => any ? E : never>[0] extends infer P ? Omit<P, "input" | "output" | "expected"> : never;

export { type ChatMessageOrPlaceholder, ChatMessageType, ChatPromptClient, type CreateChatPromptBodyWithPlaceholders, DatasetManager, type Evaluation, type Evaluator, type EvaluatorParams, type ExperimentItem, type ExperimentItemResult, ExperimentManager, type ExperimentParams, type ExperimentResult, type ExperimentTask, type ExperimentTaskParams, type FetchedDataset, type LangchainMessagesPlaceholder, LangfuseClient, type LangfuseClientParams, type LangfuseMediaResolveMediaReferencesParams, type LinkDatasetItemFunction, MediaManager, PromptManager, RegressionError, type RegressionErrorMessageOptions, type RegressionErrorMetricOptions, type RegressionErrorOptions, type RunEvaluator, type RunEvaluatorParams, type RunExperimentOnDataset, RunnerContext, type RunnerContextExperimentParams, type RunnerContextOptions, ScoreManager, TextPromptClient, createEvaluatorFromAutoevals };

What the script does:
- Initializes OpenTelemetry with `LangfuseSpanProcessor` before running the experiment.
- Uses `@langfuse/client`, `@langfuse/otel`, and `@opentelemetry/sdk-node`.
- Runs `langfuse.experiment.run({...})` over an in-process local dataset of exactly 3 items.
- Uses a task function that returns `input.toUpperCase()` without calling any external LLM provider.
- Adds item-level evaluators named `accuracy` and `response_length`.
- Flushes/shuts down Langfuse and OpenTelemetry before writing `output.log`

- `/home/user/myproject/package.json`
- `/home/user/myproject/tsconfig.json`
- `/home/user/myproject/src/index.ts`
- `/home/user/myproject/output.log`

What the script does:
- Initializes OpenTelemetry with `LangfuseSpanProcessor` before running the experiment.
- Uses `@langfuse/client`, `@langfuse/otel`, and `@opentelemetry/sdk-node`.
- Runs `langfuse.experiment.run({...})` over an in-process local dataset of exactly 3 items.
- Uses a task function that returns `input.toUpperCase()` without calling any external LLM provider.
- Adds item-level evaluators named `accuracy` and `response_length`.
- Flushes/shuts down Langfuse and OpenTelemetry before writing `output.log`.

The experiment ran successfully with name:
`harbor-experiment-zr-b46bzsv`

`output.log` contains exactly one experiment-name line and three trace ID lines:
- `a760279a1e0986525ce087cb6f0b345b`
- `8fb277962b33890afd5f26b3af03faf2`
- `4f755c39c1f35ba03ff6d762ece0b825`

I also verified through the Langfuse API that all three recorded traces exist and each
